name: Eastern Lawa

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Eastern Lawa (east2330).

notes: Austroasiatic (Palaungic, Waic) language of northern Thailand, written with the Thai script. The Thai abugida encodes syllables through consonant symbols carrying an inherent vowel, separate pre-, post-, above- and below-base vowel signs, consonant-class distinctions and tone marks whose phonetic value depends on the consonant class and syllable structure. Recovering a segmental IPA reading for Lawa spellings therefore requires a per-word pronunciation lexicon that the engine does not bundle, so grapheme->IPA coverage is lexicon-dependent and effectively zero without that stratum; no alphabetic letter->IPA table is asserted. Lawa is tonal/registral, and tone and register are left unencoded.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Eastern Lawa (east2330)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
